Commodities oil unduing in the crate and Frosting
Right concerning the area from 501 and over 502;

None of these candidates has spent or collected any money, and it seems therefore is not responsible for filling Form 501 and Form 502, according to your guidelines. I'm only writing this to help make certain that there are no fines from your commission.

Each of the above candidates filed a form 470 campaign statement in the middle of March in Sacramento stating that less than $4000.00 will be spent. How do forms 470 relate to form 501 and 502? In the future, for example, what happens if a $25 donation arrives to one of our poor candidates. How can that candidate comply with the law if no bank will open a checking account and print checks for $25? This is a real situation in the Peace and Freedom Party.

Sincerely,

John Homigfeld
Shirley Isaacson
SHIRLEY ISAACSON

30

In the future, for example, what happens if a donation arrives to one of our poor candidates. How can that candidate comply with the law if no bank will open a checking account and print checks for $25 ? This is a real situation in the Peace and Freedom Party.

Sincerely,

John Homefeld

sections.question:
None of these candidates has spent or collected any money, and it seems therefore is not responsible for filling Form 501 and Form 502, according to your guidelines. I'm only writing this to help make certain that there are no fines from your commission.

Each of the above candidates filed a form 470 Campaign statement in the middle of March in Sacramento stating that less than $1,000 will be spent. How do forms 470 relate to form 501 and 502?

In the future, for example, what happens if a donation arrives to one of our poor candidates. How can that candidate comply with the law if no bank will open a checking account and print checks for $25? This is a real situation in the Peace and Freedom Party.
sections.question_synthetic: The requestor seeks clarification on whether 15 Peace and Freedom Party candidates who have spent and collected no money must file Forms 501 and 502, how Form 470 filings relate to Forms 501 and 502, and how candidates can comply with campaign finance law if banks will not open accounts for minimal donations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section present. Document appears to be a letter of inquiry to the FPPC rather than advice from the FPPC. Text is repeated twice in source document (appears to be a scanning artifact).

In the future, for example, what happens if a donation arrives to one of our poor candidates. How can that candidate comply with the law if no bank will open a checking account and print checks for $25? This is a real situation in the Peace and Freedom Party.
embedding.first_500_words: June 6, 1990 John Hengisfeld 350 S. Fuller Ave #2B Los Angeles, CA 90036 (213) 939-8844, (213) 939-6314 (type) Ms. Alice Hughes California Fair Political Practices Commission: I am replying for a whole group of Candidates all running in the Peace and Freedom Party concerning the Form 501 and Form 502; they are Shirley R. Isaacson, Controller Elizabeth Nakano, Treasurer Julie Faust, Secretary of State Nancy Lawrence, State Board of Equalization #2 Salomea Hengisfeld, State Board of Equalization #4 Mage Hinds, State Senator #22 Ivan Klasimoff, State Senator #28 Charles Naborgier, Assembly #38 David Velasco, Assembly #41 John Lindblad, Assembly #43 Timothy Burdick, Assembly #44 Dwen Staley, Assembly #45 Robert Tavos, Assembly #46 Alice Miles, Assembly #49 Michael Long, Assembly #50. None of these candidates has spent or collected any money, and it seems therefore is not responsible for filling Form 501 and Form 502, according to your guidelines. I'm only writing this to help make certain that there are no fines from your commission. Each of the above candidates filed a form 470 Campaign statement in the middle of March in Sacramento stating that less than $1,000 will be spent. How do forms 470 relate to form 501 and 502? In the future, for example, what happens if a donation arrives to one of our poor candidates. How can that candidate comply with the law if no bank will open a checking account and print checks for $25? This is a real situation in the Peace and Freedom Party. Sincerely, John Homigfeld Shirley Isaacson SHIRLEY ISAACSON None of these candidates has spent or collected any money, and it seems therefore is not responsible for filling Form 501 and Form 502, according to your guidelines. I'm only writing this to help make certain that there are no fines from your commission. Each of the above candidates filed a form 470 Campaign statement in the middle of March in Sacramento stating that less than $1,000 will be spent. How do forms 470 relate to form 501 and 502? In the future, for example, what happens if a donation arrives to one of our poor candidates. embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from John Hengisfeld on behalf of 15 Peace and Freedom Party candidates asking about Form 501 and 502 filing obligations for candidates with no campaign activity and seeking guidance on compliance issues.
